refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly specify the latency percentile and network conditions to clarify acceptance criteria and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency over the network link during teleoperation sessions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms under defined network conditions.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency on the vehicle side.
FR2: Minimize communication latency on the teleoperation control side.
FR3: Ensure reliable and low-latency network link between vehicle and teleoperation modules.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize vehicle-side telemetry data encoding and transmission protocols.
DP2: Optimize teleoperation control command processing and tasking dispatch.
DP3: Use dedicated low-latency network link with QoS and redundancy.
DP4: Implement latency monitoring and adaptive buffering mechanisms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Combination of DP1, DP2, and DP3 to address latency sources at vehicle, teleoperation, and network link layers.

potential_sources: Telemetry data encoding affects network load and latency.
Tasking dispatch timing impacts command responsiveness.
Network link QoS settings influence both telemetry and tasking latency.
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized with clear interface boundaries.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Risk of latency spikes due to unexpected network congestion.
Assumes vehicle and teleoperation modules can be optimized independently.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding explicit DP for latency monitoring to enable dynamic adaptation.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Implements teleoperation control command processing, tasking dispatch, and telemetry data handling on the operator side.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Telemetry data flows bidirectionally between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Control commands and tasking messages flow bidirectionally between Teleoperation and Vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection supporting telemetry and tasking data exchange.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level system module; contains all but too broad for focused latency control.
Contains vehicle-side telemetry encoding and transmission; critical for latency.
Contains teleoperation control processing and tasking dispatch; central to latency management.
Primarily safety alerting; minimal role in latency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency-sensitive teleoperation logic and tasking dispatch are best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and enable focused optimization.
containment_risks: Potential latency impact from Vehicle module telemetry encoding if not tightly coordinated.
Network link variability outside module control may affect latency despite module optimization.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Coordinate latency optimization strategies explicitly between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules; consider latency monitoring as a cross-module service.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well decomposed and the selected DPs logically address the latency sources with clear mapping.

diffusion_risks: Latency optimization logic spread between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause coordination challenges.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module is the primary owner of latency-sensitive control logic; Vehicle module handles telemetry encoding. Interfaces are well defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end across the assigned modules using telemetry and network link interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear ownership of latency-related functions between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

recommended_revisions: Add explicit latency monitoring DP and consider its IM placement.
Define coordination protocols between Vehicle and Teleoperation for latency optimization.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, adding explicit latency monitoring and cross-module coordination will strengthen the design and reduce risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1.1: Encode telemetry data with minimal overhead on vehicle side.
FR1.2: Transmit telemetry data with minimal delay over network.
FR2.1: Process teleoperation commands with minimal queuing delay.
FR2.2: Dispatch tasking commands promptly to vehicle.
FR3.1: Maintain network link QoS and redundancy.
FR4: Monitor latency metrics and adapt buffering dynamically.

revise.why: To explicitly include latency monitoring and adaptive mechanisms as part of the solution.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Vehicle and Teleoperation modules can be optimized independently.
open_risks: Latency spikes due to network variability.
Coordination challenges between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
evidence_needed: Latency measurement data under operational network conditions.
Verification of latency monitoring and adaptation effectiveness.
